text:
Two falsifiable targets, adapted to tenex-edge (these are the case-study experiments, §7):

1. **Diagnosis metric.** An off-the-shelf model, given *only* the serialized commit/trace stream around a **seeded** lifecycle bug (a subscription that fails to close; a status that republishes when it shouldn't), identifies root cause with ≥90% accuracy — versus its baseline from tenex-edge's raw `tracing` logs of the same incident. The delta is the publishable Story-3 result.
2. **Safety invariant.** On any surface at `authoritative` or above, **no host effect executes without a Trellis plan/frame having been produced first** — enforced structurally by the bypass ratchet (§4.5), not by convention. This is the operational form of "never gamble."
 [...] 
**Experiment A — the diagnosis metric (Story 3).** Seed a lifecycle bug (revert the refcount so a shared channel closes on first-owner departure, or disable status dedup). Run the incident twice: give a model (a) the serialized commit/trace stream + labels, (b) the raw `tracing` logs. Measure root-cause accuracy. Target ≥90% from receipts vs the log baseline; the delta is the publishable result and the literal demonstration of "diagnosis becomes lookup, not abduction."

**Experiment B — the safety invariant (Story 1).** Show, via the bypass ratchet + `probe simulate`, that on authoritative surfaces every effect was preceded by a previewed plan — "no effect without a machine-reviewable plan first," enforced structurally.

**Beachhead evidence tenex-edge already owns (Story 2 — lead with this).** The refcounted, close-on-last-owner subscription reconciler *already fixed a real unbounded leak* the old aggregate `SubscriptionRegistry` could not tear down (`subscriptions/mod.rs:1-25`, adoption doc §surfaces). `probe stats` quantifies it live: opens/closes/coalesced/`live_now`, "N closes emitted, 0 leaks." This is the "you have leaked subscriptions right now" story, told by a shipping consumer with numbers — the honest version NMP's shadow-mode adoption can't yet tell because it isn't authoritative.
